text:
In the sleepy town of Willow Creek lived a lonely old man named Harold. He spent his days reading ancient tomes in the attic—books dense with lore and magic. Once every summer, Harold hosted a little feast for the owls perched outside his window. He had made them comfortable nests within his oversized hat.

This particular summer was peculiar. As Harold sat under his favorite oak reading aloud from a forgotten epic, a flash of light pierced the leaves. When he opened his eyes, a young woman stood there. She was beautiful, with cascading blonde hair like a golden waterfall.

"Daisy?" he gasped.

She nodded. "Yes. I've come looking for a book."

"A story?"

One of my own," she said enthusiastically. "A tale from before we had laws. Listen!"

Harold leaned forward eagerly.

"Narrative tales were our currency of romance," Daisy began. "I'm here to find my last volume before all knowledge vanished.”

Harold felt thrilled.
